what_it_does: Reads the full mined history corpus (find_corpus projection='history'), tallies the pattern_tag of each exchange record plus signal fields (tim_correction, my_error, frustration, bug_fix, needs_tim), embeds the distinct tags via the fabric embeddings transport, greedily clusters them by cosine >= 0.78 weighted by recurrence count, attaches a representative correction/error example per cluster, and writes the top clusters to .build/g13/clusters.json. Returns a summary with the top-10 named patterns.
observations: Operates on records persisted under projection 'history' via Suite.find_corpus; pulls output dict from store.get_content(r['cas']).
THRESHOLD = 0.78 with an inline comment that it is conservative ('under-merge > over-merge').
Hand-rolled cosine similarity function (cos) rather than a library.
Greedy weight-ordered clustering: heaviest tag seeds a cluster, later tags join the first cluster whose seed vector clears the threshold.
Collects per-cluster examples only from tags that carry a tim_correction or my_error, truncated to 160 chars.
chdir + sys.path.insert to /home/tim/company; embeds via DEFAULT_EMBED_MODEL from fabric.config.
Output JSON caps the persisted clusters at top 30; return value caps at top 10.
Docstring frames the script as 'THE FLOOR: read + embed + cluster — proposes/reports only' and explicitly defers the report (G13-PATTERN-REPORT.md) to the lead as judgment work.
standouts: Self-described as 'the system's first complete self-study of how-I-work-with-Tim' — clustering the collaboration's own failure patterns.
Deliberate separation: the script produces data only; the human/lead writes the narrative report — explicit floor-vs-judgment boundary.
conventions: Follows the Suite/NodeRegistry/FsStore construction idiom for entering the runtime from a standalone script.
Returns a dict and prints json.dumps(main()) in __main__ — the repeated script-as-callable pattern.
Uses the corpus/projection abstraction rather than reading files directly (substrate-is-truth).
concerns: Greedy single-seed clustering is order-dependent and only compares against each cluster's seed vector (not centroid/all members), so merge quality hinges on the seed; the docstring acknowledges conservatism but not this asymmetry.
Embeds short kebab-case tags (not the underlying exchange text), so cosine clustering operates on terse labels — sensitivity noted only as 'spot-checked'.
notes: Deterministic tally + embed + greedy-cluster of pattern_tags over the mined history corpus; emits clusters.json, leaves the interpretive report to a human lead.
questions: Where do pattern_tag and the signal fields get written into the history records (which role/miner produces them)?
What is DEFAULT_EMBED_MODEL concretely, and is the 0.78 threshold tuned to it specifically?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — docstring states read+embed+cluster as a propose/report-only floor, and the code does exactly that, writing data and explicitly not the report.
apparent_themes: Self-study / introspection of the agent-operator collaboration
Floor-vs-judgment separation (deterministic data work vs interpretive reporting)
Substrate-is-truth (corpus as the source, not a parallel state file)
intention_signals: Trying to turn the mined dialogue history into a weighted map of recurring collaboration patterns/failures so the system can see, empirically, how it works with Tim — without fabricating the interpretation in code.
novelty: Clustering of an agent's own conversational failure-pattern tags, weighted by recurrence, framed as a self-mirror that confirms rather than invents.
contribution: Contributes the clustering engine that condenses the mined history corpus into weighted named pattern groups, plus the explicit floor/report boundary that keeps interpretation out of the deterministic step.
summary_for_embedding: A Python script that tallies, embeds, and greedily cosine-clusters the pattern_tags of mined history-corpus exchanges into weighted named collaboration-pattern groups written to clusters.json.
intention_for_embedding: To produce a deterministic, weighted self-study of recurring how-the-agent-works-with-Tim patterns from the full mined dialogue history, leaving the narrative interpretation to a human lead.
